# terrible_check_point_mgmt_cp_mgmt_service_other (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Object name.

### Optional

- `accept_replies` (Boolean) Specifies whether Other Service replies are to be accepted.
- `action` (String) Contains an INSPECT expression that defines the action to take if a rule containing this service is matched. Example, set r_mhandler &open_ssl_handler sets a handler on the connection. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `aggressive_aging` (String) Sets short (aggressive) timeouts for idle connections.
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `color` (String) Color of the object. Should be one of existing colors.
- `comments` (String) Comments string.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `details_level` (String) The level of detail for some of the fields in the response can vary from showing only the UID value of the object to a fully detailed representation of the object.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `groups` (String) Collection of group identifiers.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `ignore_warnings` (Boolean) Apply changes ignoring warnings.
- `ip_protocol` (Number) IP protocol number.
- `keep_connections_open_after_policy_installation` (Boolean) Keep connections open after policy has been installed even if they are not allowed under the new policy. This overrides the settings in the Connection Persistence page. If you change this property, the change will not affect open connections, but only future connections.
- `match` (String) Contains an INSPECT expression that defines the matching criteria. The connection is examined against the expression during the first packet. Example, tcp, dport = 21, direction = 0 matches incoming FTP control connections. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `match_for_any` (Boolean) Indicates whether this service is used when 'Any' is set as the rule's service and there are several service objects with the same source port and protocol.
- `override_default_settings` (Boolean) Indicates whether this service is a Data Domain service which has been overridden. Available from R80.10 management version.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `session_timeout` (Number) Time (in seconds) before the session times out.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `sync_connections_on_cluster` (Boolean) Enables state-synchronized High Availability or Load Sharing on a ClusterXL or OPSEC-certified cluster.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `use_default_session_timeout` (Boolean) Use default virtual session timeout. Available from R80.10 management version.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `cp_mgmt_service_other` (String) The checkpoint object created or updated.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
